title: April 2024 Monthly Manager’s Report
summary: April 9, 2024 SUMMARY I hope everyone had a joyful Easter. Nevertheless, this year, numerous members and staff on the north end of the system faced the impact of a powerful windstorm. The relentless gusts caused notable damage to power lines, poles and other equipment that resulted in hundreds of outages and left thousands of members in the dark. Nearly 95 percent of these outages were caused by hazard trees outside the right-of-way corridors. The saying “out like a lion and in like a lamb” proved to be quite fitting. March & April traditionally prove to be busy months for us! We’re now in the final stages of planning for our Annual Meeting, a significant event where we come together as a cooperative to discuss important matters, celebrate our achievements, and make decisions for the future of HEA. Additionally, we are closely monitoring various energy-related discussions in Juneau that may have implications for us. Meanwhile, we are preparing for the upcoming construction season which...

title: TRUST ANNOUNCES $1M IN GRANT AWARDS
summary: Anchorage, Alaska – The Alaska Mental Health Trust Authority (Trust) awarded $1,021,948 in grants to beneficiary-serving organizations in the third quarter of fiscal year 2024. Trust grants are awarded year-round for capacity building, direct service, outreach, planning, capital, and workforce initiatives, as well as to support conferences, seminars, and training for organizations that serve Trus...

title: SNC Moose Tag Survey
summary: Sitnasuak Native Corporation (SNC) is conducting a survey to better understand moose hunting and tag usage in the Nome area. title: Viking boys’ head coach Rick Brock is the Division 2A Coach of the Year
summary: Petersburg High School’s varsity boys’ basketball coach, Rick Brock, is the Division 2A Coach of the Year. The Alaska Association of Basketball Coaches, or AABC. in late March named him for the honor at the state high school basketball tournament in Anchorage. Last week, Brock returned to Anchorage...

title: Bill that aims to loosen telehealth restrictions passes Alaska Senate
summary: The Senate Finance Room in the Alaska Capitol. (Photo by Skip Gray/360 North) Many Alaskans have to travel a long way for certain healthcare treatments — but a bill in the state House aims to close that distance by expanding the list of providers Alaskans can see without needing to leave home. Emily Nenon is the government relations director for the American Cancer Society. She’s been working to open up venues for telehealth for several years. Telehealth allows patients to meet with their healthcare providers online instead of in person. “I love talking about telehealth, because there’s so much potential in our state,” s...
